english: posite meaning: bág-yód(-pa) (bag yod (-pa)) reverence, fear; shame, often parallel to ṅo-t́sa (ngo tsha), ₒdzém-pa ('dzem pa); conscientiousness, almost religious awe; adj. conscientious; spyód-pa bág-yod-pa (spyod pa bag yod pa) conscientious dealings (pious course of life) Dom.; bág-yód-par mdzód-čig (bag yod par mdzod cig) act conscientiously, take care not to commit sin (here = do not kill) Tar. 32, 7; de bág-yód-pai pýir (de bag yod pai phyir) as he was conscientious (here = chaste) Tar. 39, 2; bag daṅ ldán-pa (bag dang ldan pa) id.; bag daṅ ldán-par mdzod (bag dang ldan par mdzod) Glr.; bág-t́sa-ba (bag tsha ba) to be afraid; bag mi t́sa (bag mi tsha) I am not afraid Mil.; sbst. fear, timidity, anxiousness Mil., Stg.; bág-t́sa méd-pa (bag tsha med pa) fearlessness Mil.; bag byéd-pa (bag byed pa) c. la (la), to fear, to dread, a person Dzl., to take care of, one's clothes Dzl.; bag-yaṅs-su (bag yangs su) (or -kyis (-kyis)) Sch. ('cura relaxata') without fear, fearlessly, coolly; bag ḱuṃs-pa (bag kums pa) Sch. to be afraid; bag ₒbébs-pa (bag 'bebs pa) to drop, abandon, cast away all fear, gżan-la (gzhan la) the dread of a person Mil. frq.; bág-pa (bag pa) Dzl. ༢༤, 15 Ms. as a vb. to be afraid, to be fearful, dé-dag bág-tu dógs-nas (de dag bag tu dogs nas) afraid lest they should take fright (another reading: brós-su (bros su)) bágs-kyis (bags kyis) with fear, with awe Mil.; bágs-kyis byéd-pa (bags kyis byed pa) to act carefully, with caution Dzl. ༢༩༢, 15; ma bags-kyis (ma bags kyis) without fear, unrestrained Dzl. ༢༤༤, 1 (Ms.; with Sch. ma (ma) is wanting, and both passages are rendered incorr.); bág-po (bag po) adj. = bág-yód-pa (bag yod pa) Cs.; bag-zóṅ (bag zong) dread, fear, anxiety Sch. -- II. inclination? passion? bág-la nyál-ba (bag la nyal ba) Was. (241) 'vanities (in Chinese: lullings into security)', the usual sinful temptations, lust, anger etc.; the etymological derivation of the term is, however, not perfectly clear; bag-méd-la nyál-ba (bag med la nyal ba), which acc. to its primary signification ought to be placed sub I, is said to imply the same. More frq. bag-čágs (bag chags) denotes passion, inclination, propensity, gen. in a bad sense, las-ṅan bag-čágs (las ngan bag chags), ṅán-pai bag-čágs (ngan pai bag chags), also occasionally without any addition, id.; bag-čágs yid-kyi lus (bag chags yid kyi lus) the 'intellectual' body of passions Thgr., v. lus (lus); less frq. in a good sense: Tar. 32, 7 = love, affection; bag-čágs bzaṅ (bag chags bzang), Mil. -- III. in compounds also for bag-pýe (bag phye) and bág-ma (bag ma).
1. vb. to be afraid, v. bag (bag) I. -- 2. purity? Cs.

(W. *bág-j́e* (*bag je*)) wheat-flour; bag-skyó (bag skyo) thin pap or porridge of meal; bag-záṅ (bag zang) thick pap, dough; bag-drón (bag dron), warm porridge; bag-sbyár (bag sbyar) paste; bags-sbyín (bags sbyin) lute, putty, a compound of meal and glue; bag-léb (bag leb), resp. bżes-bág (bzhes bag) C. a cake of bread (Hind. chapátí).

bride, lén-pa (len pa) to choose, to take frq.; bág-ma-la (bag ma la) (or bág-mar (bag mar)) lén-pa (len pa) to choose for a bride, gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to give for a bride (wife), ₒgró-ba ('gro ba), čá-ba (cha ba) Ma., *čá-če* (*cha che*) W., to become a bride, to get married; *bág-ma ṭí-te* (*bag ma ti te*) (or láṅ-te (lang te)) bór-če* (*bor che*), W. to leave the chosen bride with her parents, sometimes for years, which frequently is the case, as betrothals, from reasons of expediency, are often brought about by the parents at a very early age. The common custom is that the young man desirous of marrying proceeds to the parents of his chosen one with the 'wooing-beer', slóṅ-čaṅ (slong chang), which step however may remain yet a private affair; after some time he brings tíg-čaṅ (tig chang), the 'settling-beer', and finally bsú-čaṅ (bsu chang), the 'taking-home-beer', whereupon follows the wedding, bág-ston (bag ston), and the consummation of marriage, bza-mí byéd-pa (bza' mi byed pa). -- bag-gós (bag gos) wedding-garment; bag-grógs-mo (bag grogs mo) bride's maid Cs.; bag-zoṅ (bag zong) Cs. (prob. more correctly: rdzoṅs (rdzongs)) dowry.
a little basket for wool or clews of wool, W.
(also báxis (baxis), bóxis (boxis) etc.) Ar. بخشیش 1. fee, drink-money. -- 2. Sp. a present, alms.

1. foot-race, baṅ ni gżán-las mgyógs-pa (bang ni gzhan las mgyogs pa) to be quicker in running than another; de daṅ baṅ mnyám-par rgyúg-pa (de dang bang mnyam par rgyug pa) to run with equal swiftness as ... Pth.; baṅ rgyúg-pa (bang rgyug pa) Cs., *bhaṅ táṅ-wa* (*bhang tang wa*) C.; *baṅ túṅ- (*bang tung-)
